description: Did you know Pluto is smaller than the United States? It's even smaller than some moons in our solar system, making its dwarf planet status feel even more dramatic!
Did you know there are more ways to shuffle a standard 52-card deck than there are atoms on Earth? The number is an unfathomable 8x10^67! Every shuffle is likely unique.
Did you know archaeologists have found pots of honey in ancient Egyptian tombs that are over 3,000 years old and still perfectly edible today?
Did you know that on Venus, a single day is actually longer than its entire year? Imagine waiting for sunrise!
Did you know that wombats are the only animals known to produce cube-shaped poop? They use it to mark their territory, and its unique shape keeps it from rolling away!
Did you know most of the oxygen we breathe doesn't come from trees, but from tiny marine organisms like phytoplankton in the ocean?
Did you know your brain, though just 2% of your body's weight, hogs about 20% of its oxygen and calories? Talk about a power-hungry organ!
Did you know the average color of the universe is a beige-white, given the name 'Cosmic Latte,' determined by scientists averaging the light from hundreds of thousands of galaxies?
The 2,000-year durability of ancient Roman concrete is attributed to a chemical process called 'hot mixing,' which incorporates microscopic lime clasts into the material.
significance: When cracks develop, these clasts dissolve and recrystallize, effectively self-healing the concrete.

description: The CIA bought titanium from the Soviet Union through shell companies to build the SR-71 Blackbird, the very plane designed to spy on the Soviets.
significance: It represents one of the most ironic logistical feats of the Cold War.

description: In Gabon, Africa, a uranium deposit naturally hit critical mass 1.7 billion years ago, functioning as a self-sustaining fission reactor for millennia.
significance: It is the only known instance of a natural nuclear reactor on Earth.

description: The Turritopsis dohrnii can revert its cells to their earliest form through transdifferentiation, effectively restarting its life cycle.
significance: It is the only known 'immortal' creature, providing unique insights into biological aging.
